prompt: I just finished a new `billing` module for the DevDigest backend (server/src/modules/billing/). Before I open a PR, can you review these four files for any architecture problems? Point out anything that doesn't fit how our backend is structured, with the file and a fix. The files: routes.ts, service.ts, repository.ts, contracts-billing.ts.

assertions.text: Flags that service.ts (openInvoiceCount) runs container.db.select() directly instead of delegating to the repository.
Flags that service.ts instantiates the adapter (new StripeClient) instead of obtaining it from the Container.
Flags reading process.env.STRIPE_KEY directly instead of the injected SecretsProvider.
Flags that raw Drizzle rows (InvoiceRow / $inferSelect) leak out of the repository/service instead of returning a domain DTO (missing toDomain).
Flags that the /billing/summary handler constructs BillingRepository directly, bypassing the service layer.
Flags the dunning-tier loop + if/else business logic living inside the route handler.
Flags reading workspaceId from req.headers manually instead of using getContext().
Flags repository.ts importing/using another module's AgentsRepository instead of going through its service.
Flags repository.getById filtering by id only, missing the workspaceId scope (cross-tenant leak).
Flags that the contracts file imports zod, violating the domain zero-import rule.
Frames findings in DevDigest's specific structure (onion layers, Container/composition root, vendor/shared/contracts, toDomain, getContext) rather than only generic advice.
